gene_region.label: NEUROG2 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical NEUROG2 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: NEUROG2 encodes a somatosensory gene tuning touch, pain, itch, temperature, mechanosensation, proprioception, or interoceptive sensory signaling. This topic bundle emphasizes human sensory biology, with interpretation centered on visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local NEUROG2 bundle is oriented toward human sensory biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: NEUROG2 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Human senses, sensory transduction, and sensory-neuron research context
Vision, hearing, balance, smell, taste, touch, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoception pathway biology
Candidate-gene triage where sensory organ, receptor class, channel gating, tissue expression, and phenotype timing change interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: NEUROG2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for NEUROG2.
Because NEUROG2 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use NEUROG2 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.

Variant-plus-methylation review for visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.

biorender_visuals.label: NEUROG2 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: NEUROG2: human sensory biology via visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a NEUROG2 human sensory biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
